summary: Verified ADR 0154 live on the production Windmill control plane after repairing OpenBao loopback exposure, aligning the Windmill runtime to the winning `windmill_admin` PostgreSQL credential, granting `CREATEROLE` for Windmill-managed secondary roles, and converging the repo-managed lane scheduler plus watchdog scripts and schedules into workspace `lv3`.

verification.observed: `curl -s http://100.64.0.1:8005/api/version` returned `CE v1.662.0` from the production control path on 2026-03-25.
Windmill workspace `lv3` returned the repo-managed `f/lv3/lane_scheduler` and `f/lv3/scheduler_watchdog` scripts with the expected summaries and `python3` runtime.
Windmill workspace `lv3` listed `f/lv3/lane_scheduler_every_2s` enabled on `*/2 * * * * *` and `f/lv3/scheduler_watchdog_every_30s` enabled on `*/30 * * * * *`.

A direct PostgreSQL probe on `postgres-lv3` authenticated successfully as `windmill_admin` after the runtime secret, controller-local artifact, PostgreSQL role, and PostgreSQL-side password file were re-synchronized to the same winning credential.
`windmill_admin` now has `CREATEROLE`, which allowed the production Windmill runtime to recover its `custom_instance_user` path and serve workspace-backed schedule plus script endpoints.

notes: The first `make live-apply-service service=windmill env=production` attempt exposed pre-existing OpenBao loopback drift on `docker-runtime-lv3`; the container was healthy but missing the expected `127.0.0.1:8201` publish.

The final verified state was achieved from `main` after repairing OpenBao publication, granting `CREATEROLE` to `windmill_admin`, re-synchronizing the PostgreSQL password across the runtime and database hosts, then seeding the ADR 0154 scripts through the live Windmill API and forcing the schedule enabled flags to the repo-declared state through PostgreSQL because the existing Windmill schedule update path did not flip `enabled` for pre-existing rows.
